danger_signals.visual_form: Possessed and corrupted humanoid enemies throughout environments
danger_signals.meaning: Areas infested by Serpent Rider corruption; player must fight through to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Hell's Maw environment with hellish visual aesthetic
danger_signals.meaning: Dangerous supernatural zone guarded by powerful Iron Liches; high-threat area
Gateway guardians and major threats; defeat required to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Portal-guarding enemy type with imposing silhouette
danger_signals.meaning: Dimensional threat entities blocking access to D'Sparil's dome

danger_signals.visual_form: Rushing water, perilous terrain in later levels
danger_signals.meaning: World-state shows environmental instability; player vulnerability beyond combat

safety_signals.meaning: Player resource for damage mitigation and survival
Defensive mitigation against world threats; status indicator of preparedness
Baseline safety; player always has means of defense

safety_signals.visual_form: Locked doors and hidden supply caches
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression pathways; player can prepare before combat encounters

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: City of the Damned (Silverspring); elvish ruins showing decay from magical war; destroyed by self-sacrifice ritual

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Possessed kings' armies; undead hordes; corrupted creatures; Iron Liches; Maulotaurs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hell's Maw (demonic breeding grounds); D'Sparil's Dome; corrupted landscape; undead infestations

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Royal heraldry corrupted by Serpent Rider magic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mindless puppet armies; corrupted military forces
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Parthoris kingdom territories; capital cities showing control by foreign supernatural power

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Pre-game: Serpent Riders possess seven kings
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Kingdoms show signs of magical corruption; armies appear unnatural and mindless
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Armies act as unified puppet forces; lack of independent decision-making; aggressive expansion

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Sidhe elves resist and perform destruction ritual
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: City of the Damned shows ruined architecture; landscape displays ritual scars and magical desolation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Elves retreat; world becomes more hostile; power vacuum created for Serpent Riders to consolidate control

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Hell's Maw breached and demonic forces released
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hellish environment with demonic aesthetics; undead hordes infesting locations; supernatural corruption spreading
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased enemy density; enemies become more aggressive; environmental hazards intensify

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: D'Sparil's Dome environment changes; portal effects diminish; world shows aftermath of conflict
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Remaining enemies become less coordinated; player achieves temporary victory but faces perilous return journey

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-game: Parthoris in disarray despite victory
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: World remains destabilized; environment shows ongoing chaos and disorder
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Suggests cyclical threat; victory incomplete; future conflict inevitable

environmental_storytelling.example: City of the Damned (Silverspring) shows elvish civilization destroyed; fallen architecture communicates cultural loss and magical devastation
Undead hordes occupy destroyed Sidhe lands; Iron Liches guard Hell's Maw gateway; Maulotaurs protect D'Sparil's inner sanctum—spatial hierarchy reflects narrative threat escalation
Rushing water and perilous terrain in later levels show world becoming more hostile and unstable as player approaches center of corruption
Interactive rushing water and environmental objects demonstrate active, hostile world responding to player presence
Evil laughter, chains rattling, distant bells, water dripping create psychological atmosphere of haunting and corruption; reinforces magical invasion theme
Progression from destroyed Sidhe capital → demonic breeding grounds → D'Sparil's dome creates narrative journey through zones of increasing corruption

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Act as unified puppet forces; command corrupted armies; lack autonomous decision-making; aggressive toward Sidhe
Perform desperate ritual to sever king's natural power; accept weakening of own abilities as cost; retreat to preserve remnants
Ventures through increasingly dangerous zones; fights undead hordes; pursues D'Sparil through Hell's Maw and into his dome; refuses surrender despite perilous return
Station at Hell's Maw gateway; prevent passage without combat; represent major obstacle requiring tactical engagement
Guard dimensional gateway to D'Sparil's dome; force player into direct confrontation; final obstacle before reaching antagonist
Maintains magical control from protected dome; represents central threat; defeat triggers world instability and player's desperate escape

notes: Heretic uses modified Doom engine to communicate medieval-fantasy theme through environment, enemy types, and visual design. The narrative is conveyed primarily through environmental progression (city→hellscape→dome) and enemy hierarchy rather than explicit exposition. World-state changes manifest as environmental hostility escalation and enemy density increase as player approaches corruption source. The game's visual communication relies on architectural style (elvish vs. demonic), enemy aesthetic distinctiveness, and spatial level design to telegraph danger, faction identity, and narrative progression. Ambient audio enhances atmosphere of supernatural invasion and corruption throughout.
